contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 90
- Description: Editorial-tone guide + multi-LLM orchestration playbook for agentic_ai_context
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/121
  
  Details:
  Surfaced two pieces of institutional knowledge previously held only in Gary's head, during a CMO-persona conversation about a proposed truesight.me blog post on multi-model AI infrastructure.
  
  ## EDITORIAL_TONE.md
  Voice/texture guide for truesight.me/blog and agroverse.shop. Derived from a survey of the ~15 most recent blog posts on truesight.me plus live farm, cooperative, and Cacao Journey pages on agroverse.shop.
  
  Captures for each surface: voice signature, headline conventions, byline rules (e.g. 'long time contributor' not 'founder', LLM bylines normal on truesight.me), structural conventions, vocabulary palette (lean-into + avoid lists), tone calibration tables. Includes a cross-site decision rule ('Methodology, architecture, ops, philosophy -> truesight.me; specific farm/farmer/product/journey -> agroverse.shop') and a pre-publish checklist.
  
  Complementary to existing CMO_SETH_GODIN.md (strategic frame: who-for, what-change, remarkability) — EDITORIAL_TONE.md is about texture (sentence rhythm, hook patterns, word palette).
  
  ## MULTI_LLM_ORCHESTRATION.md
  Operational playbook for running a fleet of LLM 'engineers' (Claude, Copilot, Kimi, Grok, Gemini, DeepSeek, BigModel, Codex, Cursor agents) across the workspace without context lock-in, token waste, or single-model dependency.
  
  Captures four core principles:
  1. agentic_ai_context as the LLM-agnostic shared substrate
  2. Plan-as-document before implementing (resilience to interruption / token exhaustion / wrong-tier-model)
  3. Tier models like an org chart (Architect / Senior / Junior / Specialist), not a leaderboard
  4. Cost discipline as a feature, with the 2014 Singapore freelancer.com analogy as the historical thread
  
  Also documents: the handoff-prompt pattern (when/how to switch models mid-task), the optional model-poll pattern (let cheap models vote on which model should own an ambiguous task), the current fleet snapshot (mid-2026 with example assignments per tier), lineage diversity for second-opinion review (Claude reviewing Grok beats Claude reviewing Claude), and the major anti-patterns ('just throw it at Opus', restart-in-new-model without handoff, single-model dependency).
  
  Both docs include explicit 'Last refreshed' dates and refresh cadences so they don't go stale silently.
  
  PR: https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/121 (squash-merged into main).
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 150.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
